name: Very Minty, Vegetable Biryani
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 cups rice (basmati preferred), washed and drained
2 cups cauliflower, cut into small florets
recipeInstructions:
1. Pre-heat oven to 400°F.
2. Grind ginger, garlic, and 4 green chillies to a paste.
3. Add the paste to yogurt with tomatoes, cardamoms, cloves, cinnamon, turmeric, garam masala, coriander powder, red chili powder, coriander leaves, mint, and slit chilies. Mix well and set aside. Allow it to soak for 2-3 hours for best results.
4. Fry half the sliced onions in 3 tablespoons ghee until translucent. Add the rice and stir until coated with ghee. Cook until a little crisp, then add water and cover. Cook on low heat until three-fourths done. Remove from heat.
5. Removing rice off heat after it is three-quarters done will leave some fluid content that gets absorbed during baking, allowing flavors to seep in evenly and the rice to become fluffy.
6. Heat 4 tablespoons oil plus 1 tablespoon ghee. Add tez patta, fennel, star anise (essential!). Add remaining sliced onions and fry until clear. Add vegetables and stir until crisp cooked. Add yogurt mix, salt to taste, and cook down until thick and spoonable.
7. Spread rice in a large aluminum baking pan, then spread the yogurt mix on top. Repeat alternating layers of rice and yogurt mix until all ingredients are used up. Cover tightly with foil wrap by crimping foil over the edges of the pan. Bake for 30 minutes.
8. Serve with cold raita and a light curry of your choice.
